tools.name: ingest_media
tools.description: Ingest a media file (video, audio, or image) for processing. Validates the file, detects MIME type, extracts duration for video/audio, registers the asset with content-hash dedup, and enqueues an initial processing job.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a local media file (video, audio, or image)

title.description: Optional human-readable title for the media asset. Defaults to the filename.

metadata.description: Optional JSON metadata to attach to the asset (e.g., pipeline config, source info)

tools.name: media_status
tools.description: Query the processing status of one or more media assets, including per-stage progress details.

asset_id.description: ID of a specific media asset to query

file_path.description: File path to look up a media asset by its original path

tools.name: extract_keyframes
tools.description: Extract keyframes from a video asset at regular intervals using ffmpeg. Stores frame images and registers each in the database for subsequent vision analysis.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset (must be a video)

interval_seconds.description: Interval between keyframes in seconds. Default: 3

tools.name: analyze_keyframes
tools.description: Analyze extracted keyframes using Claude VLM (vision language model). Produces structured scene descriptions with subjects, actions, and context for each frame. Supports resumability — skips already-analyzed frames.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset whose keyframes to analyze

analysis_type.description: Type of analysis to perform. Default: 'scene_description'

batch_size.description: Number of keyframes to process per batch. Default: 10

tools.name: detect_events
tools.description: Detect events from a media asset's timeline segments using configurable detection rules with weighted confidence ranking. Supports built-in rule types: segment_transition (field changes between adjacent segments), short_segment (segments below a duration threshold), and attribute_match (regex matching on segment attributes). If no rules are provided, sensible defaults are used based on the event type.

asset_id.description: ID of the media asset to detect events in

detection_rules.description: Optional array of detection rule objects. Each rule has: ruleType (string: 'segment_transition', 'short_segment', or 'attribute_match'), params (object with rule-specific parameters), and weight (number: contribution to confidence score). If omitted, defaults are used based on event_type.

params.description: Rule-specific parameters (e.g., { field: 'subjects' }, { maxDurationSeconds: 5 }, { field: 'actions', pattern: 'steal|turnover' })

weight.description: Weight for this rule's contribution to the confidence score
